Peter Rabbit 2: The Runaway (2021)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $40,501,717Details
International Box Office $116,713,267
Worldwide Box Office $157,214,984
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,674,432
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $2,634,579
Total $5,309,011
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Bea, Thomas, and the rabbits have created a makeshift family, but despite his best efforts, Peter can’t seem to shake his mischievous reputation. Adventuring out of the garden, Peter finds himself in a world where his mischief is appreciated, but when his family risks everything to come looking for him, Peter must figure out what kind of bunny he wants to be.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$10,101,066 (24.9% of total gross)
Legs:4.01 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:25.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$45,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,346 opening theaters/3,346 max. theaters, 5.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $47,630,341

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: June 11th, 2021 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: March 26th, 2021 (Wide) (Australia)
April 2nd, 2021 (Wide) (New Zealand)
May 13th, 2021 (Wide) (Mexico)
May 14th, 2021 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
May 14th, 2021 (Wide) (United Arab Emirates)
Video Release: August 24th, 2021 by Sony Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some rude humor and action.
(Rating bulletin 2616 (Cert #52043), 2/12/2020)
Running Time: 93 minutes
Franchise: Peter Rabbit
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead
Plot point: Talking Animals
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Target audience: Family Movie
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Olive Bridge Entertainment, Sony Pictures Animation, Animal Logic, Columbia Pictures
Production Countries: United States, Australia, India
Languages: English

Adventuring out of the garden, Peter finds himself in a world where his mischief is appreciated, but when his family risks everything to come looking for him, Peter must figure out what kind of bunny he wants to be.
